content:
❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: The issues raised by @User123 have not been resolved.

No rush, but if it's going to take a while, consider changing the contribution status to "Work In Progress" for now. Thank you.

❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: 1. Binary output issue
2. Tests not similar to corresponding validators
3. Formatting incorrectly applied
4. Other issues pointed out by @User123 not addressed yet

[General]

- Binary output clashes are generally not allowed on Clash of Code. Please modify your clash so that the expected output is not just "BLACKBEARD" or "ANNE" (which is prone to hardcoded guesses).

- This might be better suited as a puzzle, although I'm not sure if one already exists.

- Why are piles allowed to have 0 coins initially? Please explain how these piles should be treated clearly in the problem statement; the player should not be required to infer the rules from the test cases.

[Input / Output]

- Please format "First line" and "Second line" as <<Line 1>> and <<Line 2>> respectively.

- Please format N as a [[variable]].

- Please state that A1 A2 ... AN are space-separated integers.

- Please format BLACKBEARD and ANNE as {{constants}}.

[Cases]

- Please group tests/validators of similar types together; e.g., test/validator pair where all piles are zeros, test/validator pair with many piles, test/validator pair with a single large pile, etc.

- Please include more cases where N > 5. Ideally, you should have cases with a varying range of N from 1 to 100, as your constraints suggest.

- Please vary the number of coins in each pile *randomly* in some test cases.

Thank you!

Thank you very much for your detailed review and suggestions!
I will fix all the points you mentioned.
